concept.title: XSD Restrictions, Patterns, and Type Derivation
concept.shortdesc: XSD allows you to create custom types by restricting built-in types — adding constraints like value ranges, string patterns, and length limits that go far beyond what DTDs can express.

section.title: Why restrict types?
section.p: Built-in types like xs:string and xs:integer are broad. In real applications, you need tighter constraints:

ul.li: A price should be a positive number, not any decimal
A country code should be exactly 2 uppercase letters
An email address should match a specific pattern
A product rating should be between 1 and 5

section.p: XSD allows you to derive new types from existing ones by adding restrictions (also called facets).

section.title: Restricting simple types
section.p: Create a new simple type by restricting a built-in type with facets:
section.codeblock:
<!-- A rating between 1 and 5 --> <xs:simpleType name="ratingType"> <xs:restriction base="xs:integer"> <xs:minInclusive value="1"/> <xs:maxInclusive value="5"/> </xs:restriction> </xs:simpleType>
<!-- A non-empty string of at most 100 characters --> <xs:simpleType name="titleType"> <xs:restriction base="xs:string"> <xs:minLength value="1"/> <xs:maxLength value="100"/> </xs:restriction> </xs:simpleType>
<!-- Use them in element declarations --> <xs:element name="rating" type="ratingType"/> <xs:element name="title" type="titleType"/>

section.title: Enumerations: Fixed value lists
section.p: Create a type that only allows specific values (like DTD enumerated attributes, but for element content too):
section.codeblock:
<xs:simpleType name="statusType"> <xs:restriction base="xs:string"> <xs:enumeration value="draft"/> <xs:enumeration value="review"/> <xs:enumeration value="published"/> <xs:enumeration value="archived"/> </xs:restriction> </xs:simpleType>
<xs:element name="status" type="statusType"/>

section.title: Patterns: Regular expression validation
section.p: The pattern facet uses regular expressions to constrain string values:
section.codeblock:
<!-- US phone number: (555) 123-4567 --> <xs:simpleType name="phoneType"> <xs:restriction base="xs:string"> <xs:pattern value="\([0-9]{3}\) [0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4}"/> </xs:restriction> </xs:simpleType>
<!-- ISO country code: exactly 2 uppercase letters --> <xs:simpleType name="countryCodeType"> <xs:restriction base="xs:string"> <xs:pattern value="[A-Z]{2}"/> </xs:restriction> </xs:simpleType>
<!-- ISBN-13: 13 digits with optional hyphens --> <xs:simpleType name="isbnType"> <xs:restriction base="xs:string"> <xs:pattern value="[0-9]{3}-?[0-9]-?[0-9]{2}-?[0-9]{6}-?[0-9]"/> </xs:restriction> </xs:simpleType>

section.title: Complex type extension: Building on existing types
section.p: You can create a new complex type by extending an existing one — adding new elements or attributes:
section.codeblock:
<!-- Base type --> <xs:complexType name="personType"> <xs:sequence> <xs:element name="firstName" type="xs:string"/> <xs:element name="lastName" type="xs:string"/> </xs:sequence> </xs:complexType>
<!-- Extended type: adds email --> <xs:complexType name="employeeType"> <xs:complexContent> <xs:extension base="personType"> <xs:sequence> <xs:element name="email" type="xs:string"/> <xs:element name="department" type="xs:string"/> </xs:sequence> </xs:extension> </xs:complexContent> </xs:complexType>
section.p: This is similar to class inheritance in object-oriented programming. DITA's specialization mechanism is conceptually related — new topic types extend base types with additional constraints.

section.title: Complex type restriction: Tightening existing types
section.p: You can also restrict a complex type — keeping the same structure but tightening constraints:
section.codeblock: <!-- Restricted type: lastName limited to 50 chars --> <xs:complexType name="shortNamePersonType"> <xs:complexContent> <xs:restriction base="personType"> <xs:sequence> <xs:element name="firstName" type="xs:string"/> <xs:element name="lastName"> <xs:simpleType> <xs:restriction base="xs:string"> <xs:maxLength value="50"/> </xs:restriction> </xs:simpleType> </xs:element> </xs:sequence> </xs:restriction> </xs:complexContent> </xs:complexType>
